type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: CFL installed low NOx burners in the Urea package boilers in August of 2007.  CFL conducted a stack survey on the Urea package boilers to get actual emission rates.  In previous years emission factors were used to determine NOx emissions.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: CFL conducted a stack survey on the Urea package boilers to get actual emission rates.  In previous years emission factors were used to determine CO emissions.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: CFL has changed to low methanol catalysts in the ammonia production process.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The amount of Formaldehyde reported for 2003 has increased as the total now includes the formaldehyde from the speciazation of VOCs.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Total releases of oxides of nitrogen are a combination of measured streams as well as using emission factors.  Emission factors for all the CACs that aren't measured are from the EPA section 1.4 of AP-42 for natural gas.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Particulate matter released from the Urea stack is measured during the annual compliance test for AENV.  A breakdown of PM10 and PM2.5 is not available and hence reported as TPM.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Production levels  were reduced compared to previous years due to market conditions.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The production of methanol in an ammonia plant is dependent on the age of the catalyst in the secondary reformer.  As this catalysrt ages less methanol is generated hence the lower releases in 2000 than the previous year.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Methanol releases are dependent on the age of the catalyst in the Low ì
Temperature Shifters section of the ammonia production process (as well as ì
production rates).  That is, more methanol will be released when there is aì
new charge of catalyst.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Estimated releases for ammonia for 1998 - 2000 are based on forecast ì
production rates.

type.fr: Nature des autres rejets non ponctuels

comments.comment: Releases from building heaters located throughout the Plant.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: 2022 Source Testing Results were used to estimate NOx releases from the North Ammonia Stack and South Ammonia Stack.

type.fr: Nature des autres rejets non ponctuels

comments.comment: Release from building heaters located throughout the Plant.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Fugitive Releases and Other Non-Point Releases were not included as the methodology used in previous years could not be confirmed as a valid representation of formaldehyde releases.  Additionally, a revised methodology to estimate formaldehyde releases from natural gas combustion was used.  The previous methodology included the use of percentage based Speciated VOCs in natural gas combustion and the revised methodology includes the use of equipment specific emission factors obtained from U.S. EPA AP-42.

type.fr: Nature des autres rejets non ponctuels

comments.comment: Releases from building heaters located throughout the Plant.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Use of emission rates obtained from 2022 Sources Testing resulting in a substantial increase in CO emissions for 2022 reporting, noting that the CO emission rate for the North Ammonia Stack may be as a result of upset conditions.   Annual testing completed in 2023 will confirm this value.

type.fr: Nature des autres rejets non ponctuels

comments.comment: Release from building heaters located throughout the Plant.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Fugitive releases were not included in the 2022 submission as the methodology used in previous years could not be confirmed as a valid representation of methanol releases.  Although the methodology used to estimate Methanol releases was updated for the 2022 submission (i.e., the releases from the North Ammonia Stack, South Ammonia Stack and Granulations Scrubbers Exhaust Stack now uses Source Testing results), there was no significant change in methanol releases.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Sulphuric Acid Fugitive Releases are not included in the 2022 submission as the methodology used in previous years could not be confirmed as a valid representation of Sulphur Acid Releases.  Sulphuric Acid is used in a closed system at the Plant and without proper testing results, the total releases were deemed to be 0 t/a.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The estimation of Fugitive Releases from equipment leaks (i.e., valves, piping, etc.) and Storage and Handling Releases were not included as the methodology used in previous years could not be confirmed as a valid representation of fugitive Ammonia releases.  Revised emission rates obtained from 2022 Source Testing was used to estimate Ammonia from exhaust stacks.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: No Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) of materials containing Ammonia could be provided to accurately quantify Ammonia Disposals.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Use of revised emission factors for particulates release from flaring were obtained from Canadian Association of Petroleum Producers (CAPP) 2014.  The original methodology used emission factors obtained from U.S. EPA AP-42 for external combustion sources.  The largest sources of TPM emissions uses an emission rate obtained from source testing.  All other TPM releases are estimated using published emission factors.

type.fr: Nature des autres rejets non ponctuels

comments.comment: Releases from building heaters located throughout the Plant.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Use of revised emission factors for particulates release from flaring were obtained from Canadian Association of Petroleum Producers (CAPP) 2014.  The original methodology used emission factors obtained from U.S. EPA AP-42 for external combustion sources.  The largest source of PM10 emissions uses a TPM emission rate obtained from source testing that was scaled using data obtained from Appendix A of the Alberta Annual Emissions Inventory Report Standard and Guidance Document.  All other PM10 releases are estimated using published emission factors.

type.fr: Nature des autres rejets non ponctuels

comments.comment: Releases from building heaters located throughout the Plant.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Use of revised emission factors for particulates release from flaring were obtained from Canadian Association of Petroleum Producers (CAPP) 2014.  The original methodology used emission factors obtained from U.S. EPA AP-42 for external combustion sources.  The largest source of PM2.5 emissions uses a TPM emission rate obtained from source testing that was scaled using data obtained from Appendix A of the Alberta Annual Emissions Inventory Report Standard and Guidance Document.  All other PM2.5 releases are estimated using published emission factors.

type.fr: Nature des autres rejets non ponctuels

comments.comment: Releases from building heaters located throughout the Plant.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Although there was no significant change in VOCs emissions as compared to 2021, an updated methodology was used to estimate methanol releases from the North Ammonia Stack, South Ammonia Stack and Granulation Scrubbers Exhaust Stack.  The updated methodology used Source Testing results.  All other VOCs emissions were estimated using U.S. EPA AP-42 emission factors.  VOCs emissions from flaring were previously estimated using U.S. EPA AP-42 emission factors for external combustion equipment.  The revised methodology uses U.S. EPA AP-42 emission factors for industrial flares.  Fugitive Releases of VOCs (i.e., methanol and formaldehyde) were not included in the 2022 submission as the methodology used in previous years was not confirmed as a valid representation of VOCs releases.

type.fr: Nature des autres rejets non ponctuels

comments.comment: Releases from building heaters located throughout the Plant.
